name: Introduction to Computer Programming
description: Introduction to computer programming using Java. Topics include variables, loops, conditionals, arrays, and object-oriented design.

name: Programming Languages and Techniques I
description: Functional programming, data structures, algorithms, and principles of software design using OCaml and Java.

name: Programming Languages and Techniques II
description: Advanced data structures and algorithms, software design, testing, and program correctness.

name: Mathematical Foundations of Computer Science
description: Combinatorics, probability, logic, graph theory, and number theory with applications to computer science.

name: Introduction to Computer Systems
description: Computer organization, assembly language, C programming, memory systems, and operating system principles.

name: Introduction to Algorithms
description: Design and analysis of algorithms. Sorting, graph algorithms, dynamic programming, greedy algorithms, complexity theory.

name: Software Design/Engineering
description: Software engineering principles including design patterns, testing, version control, and agile development.

name: Internet and Web Systems
description: Web technologies, distributed systems, scalable services, HTTP, databases, cloud computing.

name: Interactive Computer Graphics
description: 3D graphics pipeline, rendering, shading, transformations, and interactive application development.

name: Machine Learning
description: Supervised and unsupervised learning, neural networks, probability theory, and statistical inference.

name: Big Data Analytics
description: Large-scale data processing, Spark, MapReduce, data pipelines, and cloud-based analytics.

name: Database and Information Systems
description: Relational and NoSQL databases, query languages, transactions, and data modeling.

name: Networked Life
description: Social networks, game theory, markets, information and epidemics. How networks shape modern society.

name: Market and Social Systems on the Internet
description: Auctions, pricing, social systems, and the economics of internet platforms.

name: Theory of Networks
description: Spectral graph theory, random graphs, network algorithms, and connectivity.

name: Calculus I
description: Limits, derivatives, integrals, and the fundamental theorem of calculus.

name: Calculus, Part I
description: An accelerated version of Calculus I. Limits, derivatives, and integrals.

name: Calculus III
description: Multivariable calculus, partial derivatives, multiple integrals, and vector calculus.

name: Probability
description: Probability theory, random variables, distributions, expectation, and limit theorems.

name: Engineering Probability
description: Probability and statistics for engineers. Random variables, estimation, and hypothesis testing.

name: Principles of Physics I
description: Mechanics, kinematics, energy, momentum, and rotational dynamics.

name: Honors Physics I
description: Honors treatment of classical mechanics with emphasis on problem solving.

name: Engineering Dynamics
description: Dynamics of particles and rigid bodies. Newton's laws, energy methods, and impulse-momentum.

name: Introduction to Law
description: Foundations of law, legal reasoning, and the American legal system.

name: Introduction to Microeconomics
description: Supply, demand, market equilibrium, consumer theory, and firm behavior.

name: Operating Systems
description: Processes, threads, scheduling, memory management, file systems, and concurrency.

name: Programming Languages
description: Language design, type systems, semantics, compilation, and interpretation.

name: Compilers
description: Lexical analysis, parsing, semantic analysis, intermediate code generation, and optimization.

name: Interactive Computer Graphics
description: Real-time rendering pipelines, WebGL, shaders, and game engine development.

name: Writing Seminar
description: Academic writing, argumentation, and research methods.
